hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The rule accepts null, empty string, empty array, and the integer zero. It only rejects the boolean false.
If any assertions fail, the output contains the list of failures but explicitly omits the 'valid\n' footer string.
In 'chain' mode, execution halts immediately upon the first assertion failure; all steps schedruled after that point are effectively skipped for that specific run chain.

context_pointers.vague_reference: match the length requirement as specified in the contract
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The length must equal the argument passed to this check, handled via string.length() and type casting if necessary.

context_pointers.vague_reference: validate a substring match per the regex field spec
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The string argument must begin with the value provided in args[0].

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_float.json.description: Accepts values that are real floating-point numbers and rejects every other type, including integers and numeric strings.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=9
message=Value "1" is not a float.

feature1_2_integer.json.description: Accepts native integer values and rejects floats, numeric strings, booleans and null.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=10
message=Value "5" is not an integer.

feature1_3_integerish.json.description: Accepts integers and strings that represent an integer without loss, and rejects fractional values, non-numeric strings, booleans and null.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=12
message=Value "5.5" is not an integer or a number castable to integer.

feature1_4_boolean.json.description: Accepts only genuine boolean values and rejects integers, strings and null.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=13
message=Value "1" is not a boolean.

feature1_5_scalar.json.description: Accepts scalar values (string, number, boolean) and rejects compound values such as arrays.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=209
message=Value "<ARRAY>" is not a scalar.

feature1_6_string.json.description: Accepts string values (including the empty string) and rejects numbers, booleans, arrays and null. The error message reports the actual type that was supplied.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=16
message=Value "5" expected to be string, type integer given.

feature1_7_numeric.json.description: Accepts numbers and numeric strings and rejects non-numeric strings and null.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=23
message=Value "abc" is not numeric.

feature1_8_digit.json.description: Accepts values whose textual form consists only of decimal digits and rejects values containing signs, separators or letters.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=11
message=Value "12.3" is not a digit.

feature2_1_not_empty.json.description: Requires a value that is not considered empty; rejects empty string, zero, null, empty array and false.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=14
message=Value "" is empty, but non empty value was expected.

feature2_2_empty.json.description: Requires a value that is considered empty; accepts empty string, zero, null and empty array, and rejects any non-empty value.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=205
message=Value "x" is not empty, but empty value was expected.

feature2_3_null.json.description: Requires the value to be null and rejects any non-null value, including empty string and zero.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=25

feature2_4_not_null.json.description: Requires the value to be anything other than null; empty string and zero are accepted.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=15
message=Value "<NULL>" is null, but non null value was expected.

feature2_5_not_blank.json.description: Requires a non-blank value: rejects the empty string and strings consisting solely of whitespace.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=27
message=Value "   " is blank, but was expected to contain a value.

feature3_1_min.json.description: Requires a number greater than or equal to a given lower bound; the bound itself is accepted.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=35

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=36
message=Number "10" was expected to be at most "5".

feature3_3_range.json.description: Requires a number within an inclusive lower and upper bound; values outside the range are rejected.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=30
message=Number "0" was expected to be at least "1" and at most "10".

feature3_4_less_than.json.description: Requires a number strictly less than a given limit; equality with the limit is rejected.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=211

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=213

feature9_1_null_or.json.description: A null-tolerant wrapper around any rule: a null value always passes, otherwise the underlying rule is applied.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=36
message=Number "10" was expected to be at most "5".

feature9_2_all.json.description: Applies a rule to every element of a collection; passes only when all elements satisfy the rule, and fails as soon as one element violates it.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=10
message=Value "x" is not an integer.

feature9_3_chain.json.description: Applies an ordered sequence of rules to a single value, stopping at the first violation. Referencing a rule that does not exist yields a normalized unknown-assertion error.

cases.expected_output: valid

invalid
code=35
message=Number "3" was expected to be at least "5".

feature9_4_lazy.json.description: Collects validation outcomes across several labelled fields and reports every failure together in an aggregated report, rather than stopping at the first error. When all fields pass, the success result is produced.

cases.expected_output: valid

The following 2 assertions failed:
1) name: Value "10" expected to be string, type integer given.
2) bio: Value "" is blank, but was expected to contain a value.

_constraints.output_rule: Dispatching mode determines execution flow
Evaluates single rule on value, producing single line output
Evaluates ordered list of steps; stops at first failure
Evaluates all assertions, aggregates all failures
Valid if value is number and frac != 0; else invalid
Valid if type is native integer and not bool; else invalid
Valid if assignable to integer without loss; else invalid
Valid if type is boolean; else invalid
Valid if type is string/number/boolean; else invalid
Valid if type is string; else invalid, report actual type
Valid if type is number or numeric string; else invalid
Valid if textual form is only decimal digits; else invalid
Valid if count > 0 or value == 0 or null? (based on test cases: rejects zero, empty string, null, empty array, false)
Valid if type is null, empty string, empty array, or zero; else invalid
Valid if type is null; else invalid
Valid if type is NOT null; else invalid
Valid if string length > 0 after trimming whitespace; else invalid

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'check' AND check is 'min' OR 'max' OR 'range'
_constraints.output_rule: Valid if numeric value satisfies comparison with args; else invalid
Valid if string length equals args[0]; else invalid
Valid if string length >= args[0]; else invalid
Valid if string length <= args[0]; else invalid
Valid if string length within [args[0], args[1]] inclusive; else invalid
Valid if string starts with args[0]; else invalid
Valid if string ends with args[0]; else invalid
Valid if args[0] is substring of value; else invalid
Valid if value matches args[0] treat as regex; else invalid
Valid if string starts with letter and contains only letters/digits; else invalid
Valid if type-coerced comparison with args[0] passes; else invalid
Valid if type-coerced comparison with args[0] fails; else invalid
Valid if value is strictly identical type and value to args[0]; else invalid
Valid if value is NOT strictly identical to args[0] (including type); else invalid
Valid if type is array; else invalid
Valid if value in args[0] array; else invalid
Valid if value not in args[0] array; else invalid

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'check' AND check=* AND value=null OR value is null-like
_constraints.output_rule: All checks return valid unless explicitly checking for null/presence
Output exactly 'valid\n'
Output three lines: 'invalid', 'code=<int>', 'message=<text>'
Output 'error=unknown_assertion' followed by 'message=', containing offending value

_constraints.input_condition: Mode is 'lazy' AND all assertions pass
_constraints.output_rule: Output exactly 'valid\n'

_constraints.input_condition: Mode is 'lazy' AND at least one assertion fails
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'The following <n> assertions failed:' then numbered list '<i>) <property_path>: <message>', then 'valid\n' or just the report lines without 'valid\n'
Second line must be 'code=<integer>', no spaces between equals and code
Third line must be 'message=<text>', no extra newlines after last character
Null is treated as a specific type (not string, not number); rejected by non-null checks, accepted by null/many emptiness checks
Valid for 'integerish' if string represents a whole integer (e.g. '10', '-5'); invalid if fractional ('5.5') or non-numeric ('abc')
Accepted by 'not_empty' (non-empty check per test cases), rejected by 'empty' (zero is empty per test cases)
Processing stops at first failing step; subsequent steps are ignored for that run
The first argument in 'args' must be a string (likely containing regex pattern wrapped in slashes or raw); engine attempts to validate against it
Report format: 'The following <count> assertions failed:' then lines '<i>) <property_path>: <message>' for each failure
